evidence: The paper explicitly uses permutation importance for Random Survival Forests (importance = "permutation") and AORSF (importance = "permute"), not impurity-based methods. Feature ranking uses voting-theory-based SAV scores that aggregate binary selection indicators (1{i in S_k}) weighted by model performance (ρ_k), not importance magnitudes. The Pareto-based determination of biomarkers selects features by the knee point of the sparsity-performance trade-off, not by importance ranking. Conclusions focus on method properties: "hEFS selected substantially fewer and more stable features than CoxLasso (~10 vs. ~60 per modality), while still maintaining comparable discrimination performance with clinical baselines (C-index ~0.54-0.60)." No Altmann, Janitza, Boruta, SHAP, PDP/ALE, or importance magnitude-based claims appear in the paper.
